factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is mixed, with deceleration in the Mining and Minerals market and improvement in the Buildings market. The deceleration is partly due to lower business volumes in certain markets and the winding down of projects without significant new work being added.

evidence.text: For the nine months ended June 26, 2015 , revenues from clients operating in the Buildings market increased $74.1 million , or 12.0% , to $691.3 million from $617.1 million for the corresponding period last year.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

evidence.text: For the nine months ended June 26, 2015 , revenues decreased $393.8 million , or 52.3% , to $359.0 million from $752.9 million for the corresponding period last year. The decreases in revenues in the fiscal 2015 periods were due to the winding down of several projects in North and South America and Australia without significant new work being added.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

evidence.text: Total revenues for the three months ended June 26, 2015 decreased... due primarily to the impact of changes in foreign exchange rates, reduced pass through revenues, and lower volumes in the Mining and Minerals, Refining - Downstream, Oil & Gas - Upstream, and the Chemicals and Polymers markets.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

factors.summary: The company is experiencing mixed volume trends across different markets. While the Buildings market is showing growth, there are significant declines in the Mining and Minerals market, as well as the Refining-Downstream sector.

evidence.text: For the three months ended June 26, 2015 , revenues from clients operating in the Buildings market increased $7.3 million , or 3.4% , to $223.3 million from $216.0 million for the corresponding period last year. For the nine months ended June 26, 2015 , revenues increased $74.1 million , or 12.0% , to $691.3 million from $617.1 million for the corresponding period last year. For the three months ended June 26, 2015 , revenues from clients operating in the Mining and Minerals market decreased $167.8 million , or 63.4% , to $97.0 million from $264.8 million for the corresponding period last year. For the nine months ended June 26, 2015 , revenues decreased $393.8 million , or 52.3% , to $359.0 million from $752.9 million for the corresponding period last year.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

evidence.text: Construction revenues... decreased $218.7 million... primarily due to the winding down of a U.S. project in the Mining and Minerals market along with the effects of the strengthening U.S. dollar.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

factors.summary: Input costs have shown mixed trends, with direct costs of contracts decreasing due to lower pass-through costs, while commodity prices, particularly crude oil and other commodities, are declining, which presents challenges for certain industries.

evidence.text: Direct costs of contracts for the third quarter of fiscal 2015 decreased $247.1 million , or 9.3% , to $2.4 billion from $2.7 billion for the corresponding period last year. Direct costs of contracts for the nine months ended June 26, 2015 decreased $0.4 billion , or 5.6% , to $7.5 billion from $7.9 billion for the corresponding period last year. Pass-through costs for the third quarter of fiscal 2015 decreased $125.9 million , or 18.0% , to $574.4 million from $700.3 million for the corresponding period last year. Pass-through costs for the nine months ended June 26, 2015 decreased $256.4 million , or 11.9% , to $1.9 billion from $2.2 billion for the corresponding period last year.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES
